summary.what: Scope timbers gate to first-parent line; add cross-agent debt env-var bypass
summary.why: Parallel agents share .timbers/ via merges; the old full-DAG gate blocked agent A on agent B's undocumented commits — wrong actor for the gate to fire on
summary.how: Added LogFirstParent to the git layer; refactored GetPendingCommits behind firstParent bool; new GetGatePendingCommits + gateStrict dropEmptyFileChanges filter; HasPendingCommits routes through the gate path; envTruthy short-circuit in hasActionablePending for TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT

notes: Considered author-based attribution (option 1 in request) and dropped it: all agents in this user's setup commit as the same git identity, so author filter would no-op. First-parent (option 2) is git-native and works regardless of identity config. Original plan was first-parent + env var only, but the regression test exposed a residual case — git merge --no-ff creates a merge commit M on the first-parent line, and M was still blocking. Added dropEmptyFileChanges as gate-only filter: clean merges and --allow-empty commits have empty file lists from git diff-tree's default combined diff, and they add no work to this branch's first-parent line. Display path keeps the conservative empty=unknown rule so timbers pending still surfaces them for awareness. Env var stays as escape hatch for the narrower case where the merge commit itself touched source (conflict resolution). Reviewer flagged misleading env-var docs and a contradictory test name — both corrected before commit.
